heading:
Lapunti Arnis
De Abanico
intro: A Filipino martial art from Cebu, Philippines — a comprehensive weapons-based system built around fast abanico (fan-pattern) strikes and a signature cross-legged stance and footwork.

characteristics.title: Abanico Strikes
characteristics.description: Rapid fan-like combinations that go around conventional blocks and defenses, striking at unexpected angles in swift succession. Honed through decades of experimentation and actual combat.

characteristics.title: Cross-Legged Footwork
characteristics.description: The only style of arnis that uses cross-legged stance and movement — rooted in Choy Li Fut Kung Fu. Enables swift gap-closing, angling against opponents, effective multi-opponent movement, and combat in tight spaces.

founders: Developed by the Caburnay family — GM Felimon Caburnay and the late Supreme Grandmaster Prudencio 'Undo' Caburnay — with significant contributions from GM Johnny Chiuten, a master of Choy Li Fut Kung Fu and Balintawak Eskrima. Officially founded in Cebu, 1972.

acronym.label: LAPUNTI — the core ideals every practitioner strives to embody:
